raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Asserting a universal causal law that attributes all suffering to mental error, functioning as a prescriptive metaphysical principle for diagnosis and remediation of experience.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "All suffering possesses an identifiable mental cause",
    "Thought is the sole sufficient and necessary origin of suffering",
    "A 'correct' mode of thinking exists that would eliminate suffering entirely",
    "No suffering arises independently from external events, biology, or randomness"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally consistent within a strict idealism or mind-only ontology but logically brittle due to the absolute quantifier 'always', which precludes exceptions without additional ad hoc qualifiers.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The framework must be adopted voluntarily by an agent seeking cognitive self-regulation, and the agent must possess sufficient agency and safety to reframe experience without external constraints or acute trauma.",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "The statement is applied to cases of structural violence, physiological disease, or involuntary conditions where reframing alone cannot alter material causes, resulting in self-blame or dismissal of external reality."
}
